market_overview.headline: Markets holding strong bullish structure despite geopolitical tensions, with both SPY and QQQ maintaining Full Bull signals above all weekly EMAs.
market_overview.detail: Both major indices are in Full Bull configuration but showing narrow EMA spreads with bearish cross risks, while 41% of top 300 stocks remain in Full Bull mode. Energy sector leading with perfect bullish participation as Iran conflict drives commodity focus.

reversal_candidates.headline: Early Reversal Signals - Bear Rallies with Bullish Cross Potential

items.note: High-quality energy pullback with normal volume suggests orderly profit-taking in Iran conflict beneficiary.

items.name: AstraZeneca PLC
items.note: Big pharma defensive play with Amazon GLP-1 partnership providing pullback entry into healthcare strength.

momentum_themes.headline: Tech Hardware and Semiconductor Acceleration
momentum_themes.detail: Electronic technology leading momentum with storage and semiconductor names showing explosive acceleration. Data storage plays like WDC and STX benefiting from AI infrastructure demand while optical networking gear surges.

sector_analysis.headline: Energy Dominance with Tech Hardware Surge

strongest.note: Perfect bullish participation as Iran conflict drives energy sector leadership with 8 pullback setups for entries.
Industrial strength with 94% bullish participation and strong momentum as supply chain themes persist.
Pipeline and energy infrastructure plays benefiting from geopolitical energy supply concerns.

weakest.note: Housing and auto weakness persisting with 80% of names in bearish structure.
Software and cloud services under pressure despite individual names like META showing reversal potential.
Consumer staples weakness as defensive rotation stalls amid geopolitical uncertainty.

sector_analysis.detail: Clear energy-driven rotation as Iran conflict escalates, with commodity and infrastructure sectors leading while rate-sensitive tech services and consumer discretionary lag. Electronic technology hardware bucking software weakness.

items.text: Both SPY and QQQ showing narrow EMA spreads with bearish cross risks as geopolitical tensions could quickly shift sentiment
Finance sector showing mixed signals with 55% bearish participation despite some individual strength in asset managers
Healthcare technology deeply oversold with names like BSX down -22% from 21W EMA suggesting potential sector capitulation
Trump's Iran war stance creating policy uncertainty with shipping disruptions through Hormuz Strait impacting supply chains

news_drivers.headline: What Moved Markets
news_drivers.summary: Iran conflict escalation dominated headlines with Trump rejecting ceasefire extension and Hormuz shipping disruptions, driving energy sector strength. Amazon's GLP-1 program launch and Fed Chair nomination debates provided additional sector-specific catalysts while geopolitical tensions surprisingly failed to derail broader market resilience.

items.text: Iran war tensions escalating with Trump rejecting ceasefire extension, driving energy and defense sector outperformance
Shipping traffic through Hormuz Strait largely halted, creating supply chain disruption premium for energy infrastructure
Amazon launched GLP-1 weight loss program targeting Wegovy market, impacting healthcare and pharma sector dynamics
Trump surprised by market resilience amid conflict, expected 20% Dow decline but indices held bullish structure
Fed Chair nomination hearing for Warsh creating policy uncertainty around future rate cut expectations
